data.title: Nektar Therapeutics prices upsized $325M public offering at $92.00 per share
data.description:
Nektar Therapeutics (NKTR) has announced the pricing of its upsized public offering of $325M in shares of its common stock, a significant increase from the initially proposed $250M.

The company is selling 3.53M shares at a price of $92.00 per share, expecting gross proceeds of about $325 million before fees and expenses. 

Nektar has also given underwriters a 30-day option to buy an additional 529,891 shares at the same price. 

This offering is scheduled to close on April 23, 2026, pending customary conditions. 

Nektar plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, which may include research, clinical development (such as Phase 3 trials for rezpegaldesleukin), and manufacturing costs for its drug candidates.

data.title: UK's inflation rose to 3.3% in March
data.description:
• The annual inflation rate in the UK rose to 3.3% in March 2026, up from 3% in each of the previous two months and in line with expectations.
  • This marks the highest reading in three months. The increase was driven in part by transport costs, which rose by 4.7%, the fastest pace since December 2022.
  • Motor fuels climbed by 4.9%. The average price of petrol increased by 8.6 pence per litre between February and March 2026 and diesel prices also rose sharply. Housing and household services costs rose 4.3% driven by a surge in domestic heating oil prices, the highest increase since September 2022. Prices also accelerated for food and non-alcoholic beverages and services. On the other hand, clothing prices fell by 0.8%, the steepest decline since March 2021.
  • The monthly CPI increased 0.7% in March.

data.title: Temenos misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Temenos AG press release (TMSNY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.90 misses by $0.39.
  • Revenue of $253M (+14.8% Y/Y) misses by $42.82M.
  • Q1-26 ARR of USD 861m, up 13% y-o-y c.c.
  • Non-IFRS Q1-26 product revenue growth of 14% c.c.
  • Non-IFRS Q1-26 subscription and SaaS up 12% c.c.
  • Non-IFRS Q1-26 maintenance up 15% c.c.
  • Non-IFRS Q1-26 EBIT up 20% c.c. with 2 points of margin expansion.
  • Q1-26 free cash flow (FCF) of USD 60m, up 22% reported.
  • FY-26 guidance and FY-28 targets reconfirmed. FY-26 non-IFRS guidance Temenos reconfirms its FY-26 guidance. The guidance for FY-26 is organic, non-IFRS, in constant currencies, except for EPS and FCF which are reported. Growth rates are versus FY-25 proforma (excluding Multifonds). ARR growth of c.12% c.c. Subscription and SaaS growth of c.9% c.c. EBIT growth of c.9% c.c.EPS growth of c.7% reported. FCF growth of c.16% reported .
  • The Company has also assumed the following for FY-26 guidance: • FY-26 tax rate expected to be between 19-21%
  • FY-28 targets Temenos reconfirms its FY-28 targets. These targets are organic and non-IFRS. • ARR of at least USD 1.23bn • EBIT of c.USD 480m • FCF of c.USD 410m

data.title: Anthropic’s Mythos model is being accessed by unauthorized users: Bloomberg
data.description:
A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic's (ANTHRO) new Mythos AI model, a technology that the company says is so powerful it can enable dangerous cyberattacks, according to a person familiar with the matter and documentation viewed by Bloomberg News.

A handful of users in a private online forum gained access to Mythos on the same day that Anthropic first announced a plan to release the model to a limited number of companies for testing purposes, said the person, who asked not to be named for fear of reprisal.

The group has been using Mythos regularly since then, though not for cybersecurity purposes, said the person, who corroborated the account with screenshots and a live demonstration of the model.

So far, Mythos, which was originally leaked last month, has been made available to some of the biggest companies in tech. It was made available in preview to roughly 50 companies and organizations that deal with critical internet infrastructure, including Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), and the Linux Foundation, according to previous reports.

Following the unveiling of Mythos, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell reportedly summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting on concerns that Mythos would usher in an era of greater cyber risk.

However, the development also comes after a report earlier this month said Anthropic could gain White House support, with the Trump administration considering giving federal agencies access to its Mythos model despite its ongoing dispute with the Pentagon.

Financial leaders in the U.K. have meanwhile reportedly started to evaluate potential threats linked to the new AI model.

data.title: Meta breaks ground on $1B+ Oklahoma data center in AI infrastructure push
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) said Tuesday it has started building a new data center in Tulsa, Oklahoma, a project worth more than $1B, as the Facebook parent accelerates efforts to expand AI infrastructure and secure computing capacity.

Like its Big Tech peers, ​Meta is locked in an intensifying race to build out massive data centers to power its ambitious superintelligence bet.

Meta is also investing more than $25M in local infrastructure improvements, including roads and water infrastructure. Meta anticipates that more than 1,000 construction workers will be onsite at the peak of construction, and the data center will support approximately 100 jobs once operational.

Meta said the ​Tulsa data center marks ​its 28th U.S. facility and 32nd worldwide.

data.title: BHP seals iron ore deal with China’s state buyer, ending months-long standoff
Nordea Bank Abp GAAP EPS of €0.36, revenue of €1.76B; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Nordea Bank Abp press release (NRDBY): Q1 GAAP EPS of €0.36.
  • Revenue of €1.76B (-3.8% Y/Y).
  • Nordea's return on equity for the quarter was 15.4%, compared with 15.7% a year ago.
  • The CET1 ratio was 15.7% at the end of the quarter, 1.9 percentage points above the current regulatory requirement. 
  • Outlook for 2026 unchanged: a return on equity of greater than 15% and a cost-to-income ratio2 of around 45%.

data.title: Target Hospitality prices 7M share secondary offering at $14.00 per share
Vodafone to roll out cybersecurity, agentic AI for SMBs via $1B Google Cloud deal
data.description:
• Vodafone (VODAF) said it will provide small business customers with advanced cybersecurity and agentic AI via its $1B strategic partnership with Google Cloud (GOOG) (GOOGL) struck in October 2024.
  • The security product will be a managed detection and response ​service enabled by Google Security Operations designed ​to protect small businesses from increasingly frequent ⁠and sophisticated cyber threats, Vodafone said on Wednesday, as per Reuters.
  • It ​will launch in Germany, in compliance with the ​region's stringent data protection standards, before rolling out across additional European markets later this year, it said.
  • The agentic AI ​product - Vodafone Business AI Concierge with Google Gemini - ​is designed to engage with a small business's customers to ‌answer ⁠inquiries and book appointments, it said. It will initially be available in Greece and Germany, Vodafone said. Pricing for both products has not been announced.
  • Fanan ​Henriques, Vodafone's ​business product ⁠and international business director, said the products would help small businesses to start ​using AI without complexity or risk, the report added.

data.title: Asia equity markets subdued; investors weigh fragile peace talks and regional earnings results
data.description:
Asian equity markets were mostly lower as market sentiment turned cautious ahead of corporate earnings and amid uncertainty over a US-Iran ceasefire extension and peace talks. 

Gold prices rose above $4,750 an ounce on Wednesday.

WTI crude futures stayed above $89 per barrel on Wednesday after rising more than 2% in the previous session.

The benchmark KOSPI fell 0.9% to around 6,330 on Wednesday. The South Korean won firmed to around 1,480 per dollar.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 0.15% to above 59,400, while the broader Topix Index fell 0.8% to 3,740 on Wednesday. The Japanese yen traded around 159.2 per dollar on Wednesday after declining for two consecutive sessions.

Japan’s trade surplus reached JPY 667.0 billion in March 2026, an increase from JPY 529.8 billion last year but below the expected JPY 1,106 billion. Exports rose 11.7% to JPY 11,003.3 billion, and imports climbed 10.9% to JPY 10,336.3 billion due to strong domestic demand from a stimulus program.

China's (SHCOMP) rose 0.26% to around 4,089 on Wednesday, hitting a fresh one-month high, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.4% to 15,040, its strongest level since December 2021, and the offshore yuan 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 1.47% to 26,180 on Wednesday, snapping a two-session winning streak as investors turned cautious amid a mixed global backdrop. 

India (SENSEX) fell 0.95% to 78,659 on Wednesday, halting a three-day winning streak and pulling back from a recent six-week high.

Moody’s Ratings slashed India’s FY2027 growth forecast to 6% from 6.8% earlier, citing weaker private consumption amid higher energy prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. 

Australia (AS51) fell 1.19% to 8,865 in Wednesday morning trade, extending a run of weak sessions. The Australian dollar held its recent decline to below $0.72.

The Westpac–Melbourne Institute Leading Economic Index inched down 0.1% month-on-month in March 2026.

In the U.S. on Monday, all three major indexes ended lower as investors worried a US-Iran peace deal would not be reached before the ceasefire expires Wednesday

U.S. stock futures advanced on Wednesday after President Donald Trump extended the current ceasefire with Iran: Dow +0.53%; S&P 500 +0.58%; Nasdaq +0.74%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).
